portfolio_status: All 9 agents have basic hindsight/* tools (Phase 1 complete)
next_phase: Phase 3: Orchestration Optimization with Advanced Hindsight Features

executive_summary.current_hindsight_depth: Phase 1 (baseline memory collection) - all agents have retain/recall/reflect access but minimal reflect() usage
executive_summary.key_insight: Documentation specialist (@doc) and orchestrator (@ben) have the highest ROI upskills. Mental models create immediate value (consistency, speed). Directive configuration unlocks quality improvements.
executive_summary.recommendation: Prioritize quick wins in Week 1-2 (doc, ben), then move to strategic priorities (research, explore-codebase, ar-director, ar-upskiller) in Week 3-4.

phase_3_roi_ranking.recommendation: PRIORITY 1 (Week 1) - Create mental models for doc standards
PRIORITY 2 (Week 1-2) - Add mental models for agent portfolio state
phase_3_roi_ranking.status: Quick Win - Depends on agent assessments

phase_3_roi_ranking.current_usage: Minimal hindsight (newly recruited, Phase 1 baseline only)
phase_3_roi_ranking.recommendation: PRIORITY 3 (Week 1-2) - Create mental models from workspace patterns
phase_3_roi_ranking.status: Quick Win - Low effort, builds script quality

phase_3_roi_ranking.recommendation: PRIORITY 4 (Week 2-3) - Mental models for specialization patterns
PRIORITY 6 (Week 3-4) - Mental models for architecture state
PRIORITY 7 (Week 4) - Mental models + directives for rigor

1_doc_documentation_specialist.role: Documentation specialist — writes clear, concise technical documentation; synthesizes research findings into living documentation

retained_operations: Periodic recall/reflect for living doc synthesis (Phase 2 feature)
current_hindsight_usage.recall_depth: Medium - recalls recent research and codebase discoveries
current_hindsight_usage.reflect_usage: Active - uses reflect for pattern synthesis across multiple sources
current_hindsight_usage.observations: Auto-generated only (not explicitly scoped or leveraged)

advanced_features_to_add.current_state: No mental models; synthesis approach varies per session
advanced_features_to_add.recommended_change: Create 2-3 mental models: (1) 'Documentation Style Guide' (source_query: 'What are our documentation standards and conventions?'), (2) 'Common Architecture Patterns' (source_query: 'What architecture patterns have been discovered across research and codebase explorations?'), (3) 'Living Doc Synthesis Framework' (source_query: 'How should we synthesize discoveries into documentation updates?')
advanced_features_to_add.why_this_matters: Mental models ensure consistency in documentation synthesis. Instead of varying approaches, @doc always has curated answers for 'What is our doc standard?' and 'What patterns should be documented?' This speeds synthesis cycles and improves quality.
implementation_steps: Create mental model: 'Documentation Style Guide' with source_query referencing existing docs, style standards, markdown conventions
Create mental model: 'Discovered Architecture Patterns' with source_query pulling from hindsight discoveries in research and exploration
Test: @doc recalls models and uses them to guide each synthesis cycle
Verify: Documentation updates show consistent style and pattern framing
advanced_features_to_add.success_metrics: 100% of synthesis cycles reference mental models; documentation style consistency improves by 40%+; synthesis time decreases 20%

advanced_features_to_add.current_state: Observations auto-generated but not explicitly scoped
advanced_features_to_add.recommended_change: Configure observation_scopes for documentation work: one scope per documentation domain (e.g., 'architecture-docs', 'guides', 'api-reference'). This creates separate observation consolidation per domain, ensuring patterns in architecture docs stay separate from patterns in guides.
advanced_features_to_add.why_this_matters: Scoped observations prevent cross-domain inference. When reflecting on 'What should architecture docs contain?', @doc gets observations specific to prior architecture doc work, not observations about guides.
implementation_steps: Define observation_scopes in hindsight configuration: per_tag
Tag all retain calls with 'doc:architecture', 'doc:guide', 'doc:api-reference'
Verify: Observations are consolidated separately per domain
advanced_features_to_add.success_metrics: Observations per domain show domain-specific patterns; synthesis accuracy by domain improves

advanced_features_to_add.feature: Directive: Higher Reflection Emphasis on Clarity
advanced_features_to_add.current_state: Using default disposition settings (balanced)
advanced_features_to_add.recommended_change: Add directive emphasizing clarity and consistency: 'When reflecting on documentation patterns, prioritize clarity and consistency over comprehensiveness. Prefer simpler, proven-success examples over edge cases.'
advanced_features_to_add.why_this_matters: Directives tune the reflect agent's reasoning. A directive emphasizing clarity prevents unnecessarily complex documentation patterns from being surfaced.
implementation_steps: Create directive: 'documentation-clarity-priority'
Set in agent configuration or reflect calls
advanced_features_to_add.success_metrics: Synthesized documentation is simpler, more consistent; user feedback on clarity improves

1_doc_documentation_specialist.why_priority_1: Highest ROI among all agents (2.67). @doc already uses reflect actively (Phase 2), so mental model implementation is quick and immediately valuable. Small effort (3 hours) for large impact (consistency, speed, quality).
1_doc_documentation_specialist.readiness_assessment: HIGH - @doc already has reflect capability; only needs mental model creation and observation scope configuration
1_doc_documentation_specialist.dependency_notes: No dependencies on other agents; can be completed independently in Week 1

2_ben_orchestrator.role: Orchestrator — analyzes requests and strategically delegates to specialist sub-agents

current_hindsight_usage.recall_depth: Low - uses recall for context-aware routing, but doesn't deeply leverage historical delegation patterns

advanced_features_to_add.current_state: Ben uses simple recall; no structured portfolio model
advanced_features_to_add.recommended_change: Create 2-3 mental models to represent portfolio state: (1) 'Agent Capabilities Matrix' (source_query: 'What are the current capabilities and specializations of each agent in the portfolio?'), (2) 'Delegation Patterns & Success Rates' (source_query: 'Which agents are most effective for which task types? What delegation patterns have highest success rates?'), (3) 'Agent Availability & Utilization' (source_query: 'Which agents are typically overloaded? Which are underutilized? What load-balancing patterns have been successful?')
advanced_features_to_add.why_this_matters: Mental models provide pre-computed routing knowledge. Instead of searching hindsight memories during each delegation decision, Ben immediately knows: 'Agent X is best for task type Y because [reason]'. This speeds routing decisions and reduces re-delegations.
implementation_steps: Create mental model: 'Agent Capabilities & Specializations'
Create mental model: 'Delegation Success Patterns'
Integrate into Ben's routing logic: before delegating, recall the relevant mental model to inform decision
Test: Measure routing decisions against mental models
advanced_features_to_add.success_metrics: Ben correctly cites agent capabilities in 95%+ of delegations; re-delegation rate decreases 30%+

advanced_features_to_add.current_state: Ben doesn't use reflect
advanced_features_to_add.recommended_change: Add reflect queries to Ben's decision framework, such as: (1) Before delegating research: 'Based on past research tasks, what patterns predict success for this research query? Is this similar to prior successful research?', (2) Periodically reflect: 'Looking at delegation patterns over the past week, what agents are underutilized? Should we recruit a specialist?', (3) Track orchestration patterns: 'Are certain task types causing delays or re-delegations? What's missing from our specialist roster?'
advanced_features_to_add.why_this_matters: Reflect enables pattern recognition at the orchestration level. Ben can spot meta-patterns like: 'Documentation tasks always require 2 cycles' or 'Research tasks need specialist context'. These insights improve delegation quality.
implementation_steps: Add reflect() calls at key decision points in Ben's delegationlogic
Store reflect results in hindsight with tags like 'orchestration:pattern:research-success'
Periodically run synthesis reflections: 'What orchestration patterns have emerged?'
Feed insights to ar-upskiller for agent improvements
advanced_features_to_add.success_metrics: Ben identifies 2+ actionable orchestration patterns per week; specialist recommendations align with identified gaps; user tasks complete faster with fewer cycles

2_ben_orchestrator.why_priority_2: High ROI (2.0) and directly impacts all downstream work. Better routing = fewer re-delegations = faster task completion across entire portfolio. Second priority because depends slightly on assessment of agent capabilities (which agents' current usage should be in the mental model?).
2_ben_orchestrator.readiness_assessment: HIGH - Ben has hindsight tools; mental models + reflect queries are straightforward additions to routing logic
2_ben_orchestrator.dependency_notes: Ideally completes after agents finish their own Phase 3 upskilling (so Ben can accurately model their capabilities), but can start concurrently with initial mental models based on current understanding

3_bash_ops_bash_operations.role: Bash Script Specialist — creates, executes, tests, debugs, and improves bash scripts with safety-first execution

retained_operations: Has hindsight tools but minimal Phase 1 usage (newly recruited)
current_hindsight_usage.recall_depth: Minimal - not yet building script pattern library

advanced_features_to_add.feature: Mental Models: Bash Error Handling Patterns
advanced_features_to_add.current_state: No retained script patterns; working from scratch each time
advanced_features_to_add.recommended_change: As workspace bash scripts accumulate, create mental models capturing proven patterns: (1) 'Error Handling Best Practices' (source_query: 'What error handling patterns and safety conventions have worked best in our bash scripts?'), (2) 'Script Quality Checklist' (source_query: 'What does a high-quality, production-ready bash script contain? What checks must pass?'), (3) 'Common Script Pitfalls & Solutions' (source_query: 'What are the most common bash script mistakes we've encountered? How do we prevent them?')
advanced_features_to_add.why_this_matters: Mental models codify script quality standards. @bash-ops can reference 'Quality Checklist' for each new script, ensuring consistent error handling, validation, and documentation.
implementation_steps: After first 5-10 bash scripts are created and tested, synthesize patterns into retain operations
Create mental model: 'Bash Error Handling Patterns'
Create mental model: 'Script Quality Checklist'
@bash-ops references these models before finalizing each script
advanced_features_to_add.success_metrics: 100% of scripts reference quality checklist; script quality consistent across sessions; fewer post-creation quality issues

advanced_features_to_add.feature: Directive: High Literalism for Exact Specifications
advanced_features_to_add.current_state: Default disposition settings
advanced_features_to_add.recommended_change: Add directive emphasizing literalism: 'When reviewing or creating bash scripts, maintain high emphasis on exact specification compliance. Scripts must match the requested functionality precisely; do not add features beyond specification. Security is paramount over convenience.'
advanced_features_to_add.why_this_matters: Directives tune reflect behavior. For bash scripts, high literalism prevents 'helpful' feature additions that aren't requested and could introduce unseen side effects.
implementation_steps: Create directive: 'bash-script-literalism-emphasis'
Set disposition toward high literalism (confirm exact spec compliance)
advanced_features_to_add.success_metrics: Scripts match specification precisely; no unexpected feature additions; security posture improves

advanced_features_to_add.current_state: Observations auto-generated but not scoped
advanced_features_to_add.recommended_change: Configure observation_scopes per script type: 'bash-script:system-admin', 'bash-script:data-processing', 'bash-script:ci-cd', etc. This ensures pattern consolidation separates admin scripts from CI/CD scripts.
advanced_features_to_add.why_this_matters: Scoped observations prevent inference across different script domains. System admin scripts and CI/CD scripts have different error patterns and priorities.
implementation_steps: Tag all retain calls with 'bash-script:' domain tags
Configure observation_scopes: per_tag
Verify: Observations consolidate separately per script type
advanced_features_to_add.success_metrics: Observations per script type show domain-specific patterns; script quality improves per domain

3_bash_ops_bash_operations.why_priority_3: Good ROI (1.67) and low effort (3 hours). @bash-ops is newly recruited and rapidly building script library, so mental models are immediately applicable. Helps new agent ramp up quickly.
3_bash_ops_bash_operations.readiness_assessment: MEDIUM - @bash-ops needs to accumulate 5-10 scripts first before mental models are valuable. Can start in parallel with other work; mental model creation happens after initial script accumulation.
3_bash_ops_bash_operations.dependency_notes: None on other agents; depends on script work accumulating in hindsight. Can be triggered after first 2-week sprint of script creation.

4_ar_director_agent_recruitment.role: HR Director — recruits new specialist agents when capability gaps are identified; leverages hindsight for smarter recruitment decisions

current_hindsight_usage.recall_depth: Medium - recalls prior agent designs to avoid duplication

advanced_features_to_add.current_state: Uses recall/reflect separately; no consolidated model of specialization best practices
advanced_features_to_add.recommended_change: Create mental models capturing recruitment wisdom: (1) 'Agent Specialization Dimensions' (source_query: 'What specialization dimensions work best for agents in our portfolio? (e.g., Domain + Responsibility, Tool Set + Scope, etc.)'), (2) 'Complementary Agent Patterns' (source_query: 'What agent relationship patterns have worked well? (e.g., orchestrator + workers, paired specialists, etc.)'), (3) 'Failed Recruitment Lessons' (source_query: 'What recruitment mistakes have we made? What agents did we try and deprecate? Why?')
advanced_features_to_add.why_this_matters: Mental models codify recruitment wisdom. Before recruiting a new agent, @ar-director immediately knows: 'What specialization dimensions work?' and 'What patterns should this new agent follow?' This prevents failed specializations and improves agent design coherence.
implementation_steps: Review prior recruitment decisions in hindsight
Create mental model: 'Agent Specialization Dimensions'
Create mental model: 'Failed Recruitment Lessons'
In recruitment workflows, reference these models before designing new agent
advanced_features_to_add.success_metrics: New agents follow proven specialization patterns in 90%+ of cases; agent design coherence improves; fewer failed specializations

advanced_features_to_add.current_state: Uses reflect separately during recruitment; no periodic coherence analysis
advanced_features_to_add.recommended_change: Add periodic reflect query: 'Looking at our current agent portfolio (9 agents), what specialization overlaps exist? What capability gaps remain? Should we consolidate any agents?' Run this query monthly to maintain portfolio coherence and identify redundancy.
advanced_features_to_add.why_this_matters: Periodic portfolio analysis prevents specialization drift. Without reflection, the agent portfolio gradually becomes bloated with overlapping agents. Monthly reflection identifies drift early.
implementation_steps: Schedule monthly portfolio coherence reflection
Run reflect: 'What specialization overlaps exist? What gaps remain?'
Share findings with Ben for orchestration adjustments
advanced_features_to_add.success_metrics: Portfolio specialization overlaps identified and addressed; no unintended agent duplications; clear gap identification for future recruitment

4_ar_director_agent_recruitment.why_priority_4: Good ROI (1.75) and improves portfolio-wide coherence. Should be completed in Week 2-3 after quick wins establish patterns that ar-director can leverage.
4_ar_director_agent_recruitment.readiness_assessment: HIGH - @ar-director already uses recall/reflect; mental models just consolidate existing patterns
4_ar_director_agent_recruitment.dependency_notes: Can run concurrently with other upskilling. Depends slightly on evaluator completing portfolio analysis to inform mental model creation, but can start with current understanding.

5_ar_upskiller_agent_upskilling.role: Agent Upskilling Specialist — improves existing agents by researching best practices and updating agent definitions

advanced_features_to_add.current_state: Uses recall/reflect separately; no consolidated upskilling methodology
advanced_features_to_add.recommended_change: Create mental models for upskilling methodology: (1) 'Generic Agent Upskilling Patterns' (source_query: 'What upskilling approaches have worked well across agent types? What sequence of improvements tends to succeed?'), (2) 'Tool Composition Evolution for Agents' (source_query: 'How do successful agents evolve their tool composition? What patterns of tool addition work best?'), (3) 'Instruction Engineering Improvements' (source_query: 'What instruction improvements have had the highest impact? What examples work best?')
advanced_features_to_add.why_this_matters: Mental models accelerate upskilling cycles. @ar-upskiller can reference 'Upskilling Patterns' to structure improvements, ensuring consistency and higher success rates.
implementation_steps: Analyze prior upskilling attempts in hindsight
Create mental model: 'Generic Agent Upskilling Patterns'
Create mental model: 'Tool Composition Evolution'
Reference these models in all future upskilling workflows
advanced_features_to_add.success_metrics: Upskilling improvements succeed in 85%+ of cases; consistent improvement methodology; agents report faster capability additions

advanced_features_to_add.feature: Directive: High Skepticism for Risk Assessment
advanced_features_to_add.current_state: Default disposition settings
advanced_features_to_add.recommended_change: Add directive emphasizing skepticism: 'When reflecting on upskilling decisions, maintain healthy skepticism. Question whether each proposed capability truly improves the agent vs. scope-creeping their responsibilities. Require strong evidence before approving tool or instruction additions.'
advanced_features_to_add.why_this_matters: Directives prevent unnecessary capability bloat. High skepticism ensures upskiller only adds capabilities with clear justification, preventing agent scope drift.
implementation_steps: Create directive: 'upskiller-skepticism-emphasis'
Set reflection disposition toward high skepticism
advanced_features_to_add.success_metrics: Agents maintain focused scope; unnecessary tool additions rejected; agent quality remains high without bloat

advanced_features_to_add.current_state: Observations auto-generated but not scoped per agent
advanced_features_to_add.recommended_change: Configure observation_scopes: per agent tag (e.g., 'world:@doc-evolution', 'world:@ben-evolution'). This creates separate observation consolidation for each agent's evolution history.
advanced_features_to_add.why_this_matters: Scoped observations track agent evolution separately. Patterns in @doc's evolution don't get mixed with patterns in @ben's evolution.
implementation_steps: Tag all upskilling retain operations with 'world:@AGENT_NAME'
Configure observation_scopes: per_tag
Verify: Observations consolidate separately per agent
advanced_features_to_add.success_metrics: Agent evolution patterns tracked separately; @ar-upskiller can query per-agent evolution history

5_ar_upskiller_agent_upskilling.why_priority_5: Good ROI (1.75) and directly supports portfolio-wide improvement cycles. Should follow quick wins so @ar-upskiller can leverage established patterns.
5_ar_upskiller_agent_upskilling.readiness_assessment: HIGH - @ar-upskiller has strong hindsight baseline; mental models consolidate existing approach
5_ar_upskiller_agent_upskilling.dependency_notes: Can run concurrent with other work. Ideally starts after evaluator completes initial assessments (which inform upskilling priorities).

current_hindsight_usage.recall_depth: Medium - recalls discovered patterns and symbol definitions

advanced_features_to_add.current_state: Discoveries retained; no consolidated architecture model
advanced_features_to_add.recommended_change: Create mental models capturing codebase understanding: (1) 'Codebase Module Structure' (source_query: 'What modules exist in this codebase? What are their responsibilities and dependencies?'), (2) 'Key Code Patterns Used' (source_query: 'What recurring code patterns and architectural styles are used throughout this codebase?'), (3) 'Symbol Relationship Map' (source_query: 'What are the key symbol dependencies and relationships in this codebase?')
advanced_features_to_add.why_this_matters: Mental models provide instant architecture context. Instead of searching for module structure each time, @explore-codebase immediately has a pre-computed understanding of the codebase's organization.

Create mental model: 'Codebase Module Structure'
Create mental model: 'Key Code Patterns'
Reference these models at start of each exploration query
advanced_features_to_add.success_metrics: Symbol resolution faster with architecture context; exploration queries answered with 30% fewer searches

advanced_features_to_add.current_state: Observations auto-generated but not scoped by symbol type
advanced_features_to_add.recommended_change: Configure observation_scopes per symbol type: 'codebase:symbol:function', 'codebase:symbol:class', 'codebase:pattern:async', etc. This creates separate observations for function patterns vs. class patterns.
advanced_features_to_add.why_this_matters: Scoped observations prevent cross-symbol-type inference. Function patterns stay separate from class patterns.

advanced_features_to_add.feature: Directive: High Literalism for Exact Implementation Reference
advanced_features_to_add.current_state: Default disposition settings
advanced_features_to_add.recommended_change: Add directive: 'When exploring code, maintain high literalism emphasis. Provide exact file paths, line numbers, and implementation details. Do not generalize or simplify code examples—preserve exact syntax and patterns as found.'
advanced_features_to_add.why_this_matters: For code exploration, exact references are critical. High literalism ensures @explore-codebase returns precise implementation details, not approximations.
implementation_steps: Create directive: 'explore-codebase-literalism'
Set high literalism emphasis
advanced_features_to_add.success_metrics: Code examples include exact file paths and line numbers; implementation details are precise; developer can immediately locate and use examples

6_explore_codebase_code_exploration.why_priority_6: Good ROI (1.4) and supports faster codebase navigation. Lower priority than orchestrator/doc because impact is more specialized (codebase understanding vs. portfolio-wide).
6_explore_codebase_code_exploration.readiness_assessment: HIGH - @explore-codebase has strong hindsight baseline; mental models consolidate discoveries
6_explore_codebase_code_exploration.dependency_notes: Can run concurrent with other work. Mental model creation requires codebase exploration to accumulate first (first week of Phase 3).

7_evaluator_agent_evaluation.role: Agent Evaluation Specialist — analyzes individual agents against best practices; identifies gaps and produces sourced recommendations

current_hindsight_usage.reflect_usage: High - reflects on portfolio-wide gap patterns

advanced_features_to_add.current_state: Uses reflect for pattern synthesis; no consolidated evaluation framework
advanced_features_to_add.recommended_change: Create mental models for evaluation discipline: (1) 'Agent Evaluation Frameworks by Type' (source_query: 'For each agent type (orchestrator, specialist, research, etc.), what are the evaluation criteria and patterns we should check?'), (2) 'Common Agent Gap Patterns' (source_query: 'What types of gaps appear repeatedly across agents? What are the most common gap categories?'), (3) 'Sourced Recommendation Standards' (source_query: 'What makes a recommendation well-sourced? What standards do we use for recommendation quality?')
advanced_features_to_add.why_this_matters: Mental models standardize evaluation rigor. All evaluations follow consistent frameworks. Recommendations are sourced consistently. Quality improves across evaluation cycles.
implementation_steps: Consolidate prior evaluation methodologies in hindsight
Create mental model: 'Agent Evaluation Frameworks by Type'
Create mental model: 'Common Agent Gap Patterns'
Reference these in all future evaluations
advanced_features_to_add.success_metrics: All evaluations follow consistent framework; recommendations sourced in 100% of cases; evaluation quality improves; recommendations accepted/implemented in 80%+ of cases

advanced_features_to_add.feature: Directive: Maximum Skepticism for Rigorous Analysis
advanced_features_to_add.current_state: Default disposition settings
advanced_features_to_add.recommended_change: Add directive: 'When evaluating agents, maintain maximum skepticism. Question every gap claim. Require strong source evidence (not opinions). Reject vague recommendations. Focus on impact and traceability. Prefer false negatives (missing a gap) over false positives (claiming gaps without evidence).'
advanced_features_to_add.why_this_matters: For evaluation, skepticism ensures rigor. Maximum skepticism prevents inflated gap counts and unsourced recommendations.
implementation_steps: Create directive: 'evaluator-maximum-skepticism'
Set disposition toward maximum skepticism in reflect operations
advanced_features_to_add.success_metrics: All recommendations sourced; gap claims have strong evidence; false positive rate near zero; recommendation credibility very high

advanced_features_to_add.current_state: Observations auto-generated but not scoped
advanced_features_to_add.recommended_change: Configure observation_scopes per agent + gap category combination: 'world:@doc-gaps:instruction-quality', 'world:@ben-gaps:tool-composition', etc. This creates separate observations for each agent's gap categories.
advanced_features_to_add.why_this_matters: Scoped observations track gap evolution per agent per category. Patterns in @doc's instruction gaps stay separate from tool composition gaps.
implementation_steps: Tag evaluation retain operations: 'world:@AGENT_NAME-gaps:CATEGORY'
Configure observation_scopes to match
Verify: Observations per agent + gap category
advanced_features_to_add.success_metrics: Gap patterns tracked separately per agent per category; evaluator can query agent-specific gap evolution

7_evaluator_agent_evaluation.why_priority_7: Good ROI (1.6), but lower priority than operational agents (ben, doc, upskiller). Assessment/analysis are important but less urgent than improving agent capabilities.
7_evaluator_agent_evaluation.readiness_assessment: HIGH - @evaluator already uses reflect actively; mental models consolidate framework
7_evaluator_agent_evaluation.dependency_notes: Can run concurrent with other work. Best after initial evaluations complete to inform mental model creation.

advanced_features_to_add.current_state: Default disposition settings
advanced_features_to_add.recommended_change: Add directive: 'When conducting research, maintain high skepticism about sources. Prefer official documentation and peer-reviewed sources. Flag opinion pieces, blogs, and unverified claims. Require strong evidence before accepting claims as facts. Distinguish clearly between consensus, emerging, and controversial approaches.'
advanced_features_to_add.why_this_matters: High skepticism ensures research quality. Prevents hallucinated research claims; ensures citations are rigorous.
implementation_steps: Create directive: 'research-source-skepticism'
Set high skepticism emphasis
advanced_features_to_add.success_metrics: Research outputs are well-sourced; source credibility is high; false research claims are rare

9_research_external_research_specialist.why_priority_9: Lowest ROI (1.0) among all agents. Research deduplication improves, but impact is localized to research domain rather than portfolio-wide. Lower priority than operational improvements.
9_research_external_research_specialist.dependency_notes: Can run concurrent with other work. Lower priority; can be deferred to Week 4-5 if timeline is tight.

week_1_quick_wins.focus: High-ROI, low-effort improvements that show immediate value
deliverables: @doc: 3 mental models (Documentation Standards, Architecture Patterns, Synthesis Framework)
Ben: 2 mental models (Agent Capabilities, Delegation Patterns)
@bash-ops: 2 mental models (Error Handling, Quality Checklist) + directive
success_criteria: Ben routing decisions cite model-based reasoning
week_1_quick_wins.notes: Quick wins establish patterns that inform later strategic work. Week 1 completion creates momentum for rest of Phase 3.

deliverables: @ar-director: 3 mental models (Specialization Dimensions, Complementary Patterns, Failed Lessons) + periodic portfolio coherence reflect
@ar-upskiller: 3 mental models (Upskilling Patterns, Tool Evolution, Instruction Engineering) + directive
success_criteria: New recruits follow established specialization patterns
week_2_priority_agents.notes: These agents directly support portfolio optimization. Week 2 completion ensures upskilling capacity ready for Phase 4.

week_3_code_and_analysis.focus: Improve codebase navigation and evaluation rigor
deliverables: @explore-codebase: 3 mental models (Module Structure, Patterns, Symbol Map) + observation scopes + directive
@evaluator: 3 mental models (Frameworks, Gap Patterns, Standards) + maximum skepticism directive + observation scopes
success_criteria: Code exploration 30% faster with architecture context
Gap patterns tracked per agent per category
week_3_code_and_analysis.notes: Analysis agents are critical for portfolio health. These upskills improve quality of all future evaluations.

week_4_research_and_polish.focus: Enhance research synthesis and handle final improvements
deliverables: @agentic-workflow-researcher: 3 mental models (Best Practices, Specialization Patterns, Orchestration Strategies) + observation scopes + directive
@research: 2 mental models (Domain Index, Confidence Assessment) + advanced tag hierarchies + directive
success_criteria: Research outputs are consistent, well-sourced, clearly structured
Pattern synthesis in agentic workflows is authoritative
week_4_research_and_polish.notes: Research enhancement completes Phase 3. These agents provide input for future agent improvements.

week_5_verification_and_tuning.focus: Test all upskills, fine-tune implementation, document learnings
deliverables: Test all 9 mental models: Are they returning correct synthesized answers?
Verify directives: Are agents following disposition guidance?
Verify observation scopes: Are patterns consolidating correctly?
Document: Mental model implementations, directive configurations, tagging schema used
Retain learnings: Document Phase 3 upskilling process and outcomes
success_criteria: All mental models tested and working correctly
Directives improving agent reasoning as intended
No regressions in agent quality or capability
week_5_verification_and_tuning.notes: Week 5 optional if Week 1-4 are on schedule. Otherwise integrate testing into concurrent weeks.

quick_wins_pattern.definition: High-ROI (>2.0), low-effort (<4 hours) improvements that are immediately implementable
quick_wins_pattern.why_it_matters: Quick wins establish momentum and demonstrate hindsight value early. Complete Week 1.
quick_wins_pattern.implementation_approach: Focus on mental model creation; minimal directive/observation scope work

mental_models_across_portfolio.pattern: Each agent gets 2-3 mental models covering core responsibilities and decision frameworks
mental_models_across_portfolio.rollout_sequence: Sequential by agent priority; mental models created as agent accumulates relevant hindsight memories
mental_models_across_portfolio.portfolio_value: Mental models become company-wide knowledge base; new agents can reference existing models to accelerate onboarding

directives_and_disposition_tuning.pattern: Directives applied selectively to agents needing specialized reasoning emphasis
types_used: Skepticism directives (research, evaluation, upskilling, agentic research) — verify claims, require evidence
Literalism directives (bash-ops, explore-codebase) — exact specifications, precise references
Clarity emphasis (doc, upskiller) — prioritize simplicity and consistency
directives_and_disposition_tuning.portfolio_value: Directives fine-tune reasoning for agent specialization; prevent common failure modes (hallucination, scope creep, ambiguity)

scope_approaches: Per-agent tracking (ar-upskiller, evaluator) — separate observation consolidation per agent
Per-domain tracking (@doc, @explore-codebase, @agentic-workflow-researcher, @research) — separate observations per research/code domain
Per-symbol-type tracking (@explore-codebase) — separate patterns for functions vs. classes
Per-script-type tracking (@bash-ops) — separate patterns for admin vs. CI/CD scripts
observation_scopes_strategy.portfolio_value: Scoped observations prevent false inference; patterns stay in appropriate domains; reflection becomes more precise

factor_1_mental_model_quality.risk: Mental models created from insufficient hindsight memory; answers are generic or incorrect
factor_1_mental_model_quality.mitigation: Delay mental model creation until agent has accumulated 10+ relevant memories. Review models after creation; allow human refinement before deployment.
factor_1_mental_model_quality.verification: Test mental model queries against hindsight; ensure responses are specific and accurate

factor_2_directive_alignment.risk: Directives contradict agent's core purpose (e.g., high skepticism for @doc harms writing confidence)
factor_2_directive_alignment.mitigation: Review each directive against agent role and responsibilities. Test with sample reflect queries before deployment.
factor_2_directive_alignment.verification: Monitor agent behavior after directive; ensure reasoning is improved, not hindered

factor_3_observation_scope_configuration.risk: Observation scopes misconfigured; patterns get consolidated incorrectly or observations are lost
factor_3_observation_scope_configuration.mitigation: Test observation_scopes with sample retain/reflect cycles before deploying to production
factor_3_observation_scope_configuration.verification: Query observations by scope; verify they consolidate correctly and reflect returns appropriate observations

factor_4_tag_consistency.risk: Inconsistent tagging across agents makes recall/reflect filtering unreliable
factor_4_tag_consistency.mitigation: Document tag schema in .github/context/hindsight-tag-schema.md; review all retain calls for tag consistency
factor_4_tag_consistency.verification: Audit hindsight memory; ensure tags follow schema; fix outliers

factor_5_hindsight_memory_hygiene.risk: Memory accumulation becomes unwieldy; recall/reflect performance degrades
factor_5_hindsight_memory_hygiene.mitigation: Establish memory retention policy; archive old observations; consolidate duplicate memories
factor_5_hindsight_memory_hygiene.verification: Monitor hindsight memory size; establish alerts for excessive growth

portfolio_coherence_analysis.specialization_overlap_assessment: No significant overlap detected. Each agent has distinct specialization. Phase 3 upskilling maintains coherence by enforcing consistent patterns.
portfolio_coherence_analysis.capability_gap_analysis: No critical gaps identified in current 9-agent portfolio. Phase 3 hindsight upskilling addresses quality/consistency gaps rather than capability gaps.

dependency_relationships.ben_depends_on: All other agents; mental models improve routing effectiveness
dependency_relationships.ar_director_depends_on: evaluator (for portfolio assessment); agentic-workflow-researcher (for recruitment patterns)
dependency_relationships.ar_upskiller_depends_on: evaluator (for gap identification); all agents (whose upskilling is being planned)
dependency_relationships.doc_depends_on: research, explore-codebase, agentic-workflow-researcher (for content to synthesize)
dependency_relationships.all_agents_depend_on: hindsight infrastructure (memory bank, consolidation, mental model API)

portfolio_coherence_analysis.recommended_rollout_sequence: Week 1: Quick wins (doc, ben, bash-ops) establish patterns. Week 2-3: Orchestration agents (ar-director, ar-upskiller) leverage established patterns. Week 4: Analysis/research agents (evaluator, agentic-workflow-researcher, research) consolidate findings. This sequence ensures dependencies are satisfied before dependent agents are upskilled.

phase_4_implications.emergent_intelligence_foundation: Phase 3 mental models become foundation for Phase 4 emergent behavior. Agents with strong mental models can teach each other.
phase_4_implications.agent_learning_loop_enablement: Phase 3 observation scopes enable Phase 4 'agent learning from experience'. Agents can recall and reflect on prior patterns to improve future decisions.
phase_4_implications.mental_model_evolution: Phase 3 static mental models can evolve in Phase 4 with 'trigger_refresh_after_consolidation' enabled, allowing continuous updates as new memories arrive
phase_4_implications.workspace_conventions_emergence: Phase 3 consistency (directive emphasis, tag schema, observation scoping) creates foundation for Phase 4 workspace-wide conventions. Agents develop shared practices automatically.

pre_phase_3_setup.task: Verify hindsight API is running and responsive
pre_phase_3_setup.notes: Check hindsight service health, test basic retain/recall
Standardize tags: research:*, codebase:*, pattern:*, memory:*, etc.
Template includes: name, source_query, tags, expected content, refresh strategy

week_1_execution.task: @doc: Create 3 mental models (Documentation Standards, Architecture Patterns, Synthesis Framework)
week_1_execution.success_criteria: Mental models created, tested, integrated into synthesis workflow

week_1_execution.task: Ben: Create 2 mental models (Agent Capabilities, Delegation Patterns)
week_1_execution.success_criteria: Models created; Ben routing logic references models in 80%+ of delegations

week_1_execution.task: @bash-ops: Create 2 mental models (Error Handling, Quality Checklist) + directive
week_1_execution.success_criteria: Models reference established bash script patterns; directive improves script literalism

week_1_execution.task: Week 1 verification: Test all Week 1 mental models
week_1_execution.success_criteria: All models return correct synthesized answers; no hallucination; models are actionable

week_2_execution.task: @ar-director: Create 3 mental models + reflect query for portfolio coherence

week_2_execution.success_criteria: Upskilling methodology consistent; directive prevents unnecessary scope creep

week_2_execution.task: Week 2 verification: Test upskilling decision quality
week_2_execution.success_criteria: Upskilling recommendations have strong justification; patterns vs. one-offs are clear

week_3_execution.task: @explore-codebase: Create 3 mental models + observation scopes + directive
week_3_execution.success_criteria: Symbol exploration faster with architecture context

week_3_execution.task: @evaluator: Create 3 mental models + maximum skepticism directive + observation scopes
week_3_execution.success_criteria: All evaluations follow consistent framework; recommendations well-sourced

week_3_execution.task: Week 3 verification: Test evaluation quality and observation consolidation
week_3_execution.success_criteria: Evaluations are rigorous; observations consolidate correctly per scope

week_4_execution.task: @agentic-workflow-researcher: Create 3 mental models + observation scopes + directive
week_4_execution.success_criteria: Research synthesis consistent; pattern library is authoritative

week_4_execution.task: @research: Create 2 mental models + advanced tag hierarchies + directive
week_4_execution.success_criteria: Research sources are traceable; duplication reduced

week_4_execution.task: Week 4 verification: Test research synthesis and tag filtering
week_4_execution.success_criteria: Research is well-sourced; tag-based filtering works correctly

week_5_validation_and_documentation.task: Final verification: Test all 9 agents' Phase 3 capabilities in operational use
week_5_validation_and_documentation.success_criteria: All mental models, directives, scopes working correctly in live delegation cycles

week_5_validation_and_documentation.notes: Document what was implemented, why, expected outcomes, and Phase 4 roadmap

week_5_validation_and_documentation.task: Hindsight retention: Retain Phase 3 learnings and outcomes
week_5_validation_and_documentation.notes: Store mental models created, directives implemented, observations scopes configured for Phase 4 reference
Summary: 24 mental models created, 9 directives configured, 6 observation scope implementations, all 9 agents upskilled

risk_1_mental_model_hallucination.description: Mental models return incorrect or generic answers if based on insufficient memory
mitigation: Require minimum 10 relevant memories before creating mental model
Test mental model queries manually before deployment
Set up monitoring to detect hallucination (compare model answers against raw memories)
risk_1_mental_model_hallucination.contingency: Mark model as 'draft' and continue refining with new memory accumulation

risk_2_hindsight_api_latency.description: Mental model or observation queries cause noticeable agent latency increase
mitigation: Use budget='low' for routine recall operations
risk_2_hindsight_api_latency.contingency: Reduce mental model complexity or defer some reflect queries to background

risk_3_tag_schema_divergence.description: Agents use inconsistent tags, making recall/reflect filtering unreliable
mitigation: Create tag schema documentation before upskilling
Audit all retain calls for compliance (automated or manual)
Provide tag examples in mental model templates
risk_3_tag_schema_divergence.contingency: Retroactively re-tag memories or create tag translation layer

risk_4_directive_backfire.description: Directive intended to improve reasoning actually harms agent quality (e.g., skepticism prevents decisiveness)
mitigation: Test directives with sample reflect queries before deployment
Monitor agent behavior changes after directive implementation
Have rollback plan (remove directive if harmful)
risk_4_directive_backfire.contingency: Disable directive, analyze failure, redesign with different approach

risk_5_observation_scope_misconfiguratio.description: Observation scopes configured incorrectly; pattern consolidation is broken or incomplete
mitigation: Test scopes with sample retain/reflect cycles
Query hindsight observations API to verify consolidation
risk_5_observation_scope_misconfiguratio.contingency: Reset observation scopes and reconfigure with corrected strategy

risk_6_timeline_compression.description: 5-week timeline is too aggressive; upskilling quality suffers
mitigation: Prioritize Week 1 quick wins; defer lower-ROI agents to Week 4-5
Parallelize work across agents (Ben/Doc/bash-ops in Week 1 can run concurrently)
Use templates to accelerate mental model creation
risk_6_timeline_compression.contingency: Extend timeline to 6-7 weeks; de-prioritize research agents (lowest ROI) to later phases

phase_3_success_indicators.measurement: Query hindsight mental models API; count created models

phase_3_success_indicators.target: Re-delegation rate decreases from baseline to <5%
phase_3_success_indicators.measurement: Compare re-delegation rate before/after Phase 3; Ben should cite routing rationale 80%+ of the time

phase_3_success_indicators.target: @doc synthesis cycles reference mental models 100% of the time
phase_3_success_indicators.measurement: Review @doc outputs; check for mental model references

phase_3_success_indicators.target: All evaluations follow consistent framework; 100% of recommendations are sourced
phase_3_success_indicators.measurement: Review evaluations; verify recommendations have source citations

phase_3_success_indicators.target: Research duplication decreases 50%; agents recall prior research before starting new investigation
phase_3_success_indicators.measurement: Track research recall operations; monitor for overlapping queries

phase_3_success_indicators.target: No new specialization overlaps detected; recruitment decisions improve
phase_3_success_indicators.measurement: Quarterly portfolio review; compare agent specializations; track recruitment success rate

phase_3_success_indicators.target: Tag schema compliance >95%; memory growth is bounded; no duplicate observations
phase_3_success_indicators.measurement: Audit hindsight memories; check tags, memory size, observation consolidation

phase_3_success_indicators.target: Agents report Phase 3 improvements help their work (qualitative feedback)
phase_3_success_indicators.measurement: Post-Phase-3 feedback survey to all agents

checklist: All 24 mental models created, tested, and integrated
Tag schema documented and in use across all agents
Portfolio-wide patterns stable (no regressions in agent quality)
phase_3_to_phase_4_transition_readiness.transition_criteria: Phase 3 is complete when all checklist items are satisfied AND agents demonstrate improved performance in their specialized domains

appendix_roi_calculations.methodology: ROI = Impact Score / Effort Hours. Impact scores (1-10) assess how much the upskilling improves agent effectiveness. Effort hours estimate time to implement (mental model creation, directive config, observation scope setup, testing).

roi_ranking.notes: Highest ROI. @doc actively uses reflect (Phase 2), so mental models are immediately valuable.

roi_ranking.quick_win_feature: Mental Models (Agent Portfolio, Delegation Patterns) + Reflect Queries
roi_ranking.notes: High ROI. Orchestrator improvement benefits entire portfolio.
Good ROI. New agent ramping up; mental models accelerate learning.
Portfolio coherence improves; prevents recruitment mistakes.

roi_ranking.strategic_feature: Mental Models (Upskilling Patterns, Tool Evolution, Instructions) + Directive (Skepticism)
roi_ranking.notes: Upskilling methodology becomes consistent and evidence-based.

roi_ranking.strategic_feature: Mental Models (Architecture, Patterns, Symbols) + Scopes + Directive
roi_ranking.notes: Code exploration faster with architecture context; more precise symbol resolution.

roi_ranking.strategic_feature: Mental Models (Frameworks, Patterns, Standards) + Directive (Skepticism) + Scopes
roi_ranking.notes: Evaluation rigor and consistency improve; critical for portfolio health.

roi_ranking.strategic_feature: Mental Models (Best Practices, Specialization, Orchestration) + Scopes + Directive
roi_ranking.notes: Research synthesis becomes more consistent; pattern library authoritative.

roi_ranking.future_feature: Mental Models (Domain Index, Confidence) + Advanced Tags + Directive
roi_ranking.notes: Lowest ROI. Research deduplication improves but impact is localized.

conclusion_and_next_steps.next_step_1: Review and approve this Phase 3 evaluation with Ben. Confirm priority sequence and timeline.
conclusion_and_next_steps.next_step_2: Coordinate with @ar-upskiller to begin Week 1 quick wins: @doc, Ben, @bash-ops mental models.
conclusion_and_next_steps.next_step_3: Monitor Weeks 1-2 execution; adjust timeline if slippage occurs.
conclusion_and_next_steps.next_step_4: Upon Phase 3 completion, transition to Phase 4: Emergent Intelligence. Focus on enabling agent learning loops with mental model evolution and observation-driven behavior improvement.
conclusion_and_next_steps.final_note: This evaluation represents systematic, sourced assessment of Phase 3 opportunities. All recommendations are grounded in Hindsight advanced features documentation and portfolio-specific needs. Implementation follows evaluator-recommended priorities (quick wins first, then strategic improvements) to maximize ROI while maintaining quality.
